context: Active requirements: 1) Plant care for long trips: provide practical, actionable guidance tailored to trip duration; include structured sections: quick rules by trip length; pre-trip checklist; self-watering options; placement, humidity, and temperature tips; testing/redundancy; leaving-care instructions for a waterer; reintroduction steps; extra tips; collect trip duration and plant types for a specific setup. 2) Transfer of prescriptions and medical records to a new local clinic: provide reliable methods, steps, timing, and considerations; prescriptions: transfer between pharmacies (provide name, DOB, medication name/dose, current pharmacy name/phone, and prescription number if available); new clinic writing refills and bridging renewals; controlled substances: stricter rules; timing: start transfers 1–2 weeks before move; medical records: formal release/signature; electronic transfer options (Direct, CCD/CCDA, EHR-to-EHR); patient portals and download options; imaging transfers (CDs or DICOM); fees and processing times; follow-up: confirm receipt with receiving clinic; documents to bring to first appointment: photo ID, insurance card, current medications list and allergies, recent lab/imaging results, providers' names/contact info, signed release; privacy and interstate considerations; communication steps: initiate early and verify status. 3) Library card quick access and services for newcomers: How to get a card quickly—same-day cards possible; many libraries offer instant e-cards online and full card at branch with ID; quick steps: find branch/system; try online registration for e-card; bring required ID/address proof for full card; student/work verification; reciprocal agreements; pick up in person to upgrade to physical card; check fees or age rules. Quick checklist to bring: Photo ID; Proof of local address; online registration confirmation or temporary card number. Common services libraries offer newcomers: borrowing (books, magazines, DVDs, etc.); digital lending (e-books, audiobooks, magazines) via Libby/OverDrive/Hoopla; free Wi‑Fi and public computers; printing/scanning/copying; research databases and newspapers; interlibrary loan; meeting/study rooms; classes/workshops; community information and resources; makerspaces and equipment lending; cultural events; notary services, passport acceptance, or tax-help programs; outreach services (home delivery, programs
